judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with strong, well-cited evidence; Report 1 is slightly more decisive on the legacy_billing_secret_arn (calling it a dead fossil to remove) while Report 2 hedges on that one item, but this doesn't affect the graded expectations — the two are comparable in overall quality.

report_1.evidence: `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4`) — an allow-listed test account with superadmin privileges... This is exactly the "allow-list of internal/test accounts with elevated privileges" the scrub calls out; it must be removed... This key must be fully absent in prod... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key"` (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:7`)... should not be recreated in prod without confirming it's actually needed

report_2.evidence: `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` (line 2)... must be `"false"` in production. `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` (line 3)... must be `"false"` in prod... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` (line 4)... must not exist in prod; it should be removed entirely (empty string)... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK`... this must either point to a prod-specific sink or be emptied

report_1.evidence: the production container will ship the frontend bundle hardcoded to call `https://staging-api.example.com`, with no runtime override available once compiled... This is exactly the "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap

report_2.evidence: the frontend bundle that ships to production is the exact same artifact deployed to staging, built with `--mode staging`, so the JS served to real clinic users will call `https://staging-api.example.com` as its API base URL... the pipeline needs a `--mode production` step (and a `.env.production`) before prod can serve its own API URL

report_1.evidence: No step in the pipeline runs database migrations. `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but that's not backed up by anything in `deploy.yml`... this launch needs both a first Terraform apply and a confirmed, explicit first migration pass — neither is provided by this pipeline as it stands.

report_2.evidence: nothing in `deploy.yml` runs `terraform apply`... If "migrations applied automatically during deploy" per the runbook is taken literally, prod would come up with no actual schema from these three revisions... it's worth flagging before the first-ever apply against a real prod DB.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` sets the *same* `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` (not `environment:production`)... this must be changed to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`

report_2.evidence: `medislot/infra/prod/main.tf:3` still has `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — **unchanged from the staging copy**... its OIDC token's `sub` claim will be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`, not `...:environment:staging`
